notes: Bug is a Windows x86-specific calling convention mismatch: 878 [LibraryImport] declarations in SkiaApi.generated.cs lack [UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })], defaulting to StdCall on x86 Windows instead of Cdecl. This causes stack corruption/overflow on every P/Invoke call. On x64 and ARM64 there is only one calling convention, so the mismatch is harmless. Tested on macOS ARM64 with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 and 3.119.2 — all P/Invoke calls succeed as expected since ARM64 has no calling convention ambiguity. Reproduction requires Windows x86 (.NET 8/9, win-x86 RID) to trigger the stack overflow. Code-level evidence confirms the root cause: 878 LibraryImport declarations with 0 UnmanagedCallConv attributes. Second reporter confirms stack overflow at SkiaApi.sk_bitmap_get_pixel_color on .NET 8 x86.
blockers: Requires Windows x86 host — calling convention mismatch (StdCall vs Cdecl) only manifests on x86 architecture where multiple calling conventions exist. macOS ARM64 has a single calling convention.
Cannot run win-x86 .NET apps on macOS — no Windows VM or x86 emulation available

reproductionSteps.description: Create console project with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 (reporter's version)
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3375 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3375 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net9.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.116.0

filesCreated.description: Console project referencing SkiaSharp 3.116.0 targeting net9.0
Repro code exercising SKBitmap.GetPixel and other P/Invoke calls that cause stack overflow on x86 Windows

reproductionSteps.description: Build and run repro with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 on macOS ARM64
reproductionSteps.output:
Arch: Arm64
OS: Darwin 25.3.0
SkiaSharp version: 3.116.0.0
GetPixel(50,50) = #ffff0000
After DrawRect, GetPixel(50,50) = #ff0000ff
Encoded PNG size: 348 bytes
SUCCESS: All SkiaSharp P/Invoke calls completed without crash

reproductionSteps.description: Build and run repro with latest stable SkiaSharp 3.119.2 on macOS ARM64
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3375-latest/Repro && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3375-latest/Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.2 && dotnet build && dotnet run

reproductionSteps.description: Verify code-level evidence: check LibraryImport declarations lack UnmanagedCallConv in generated bindings
reproductionSteps.output: LibraryImport count: 878, UnmanagedCallConv count: 0. All 878 [LibraryImport] declarations lack [UnmanagedCallConv(CallConvs = new[] { typeof(CallConvCdecl) })]. On x86 Windows, LibraryImport defaults to StdCall, while native Skia C API uses Cdecl. The DllImport fallback correctly specifies CallingConvention.Cdecl.

versionResults.notes: All P/Invoke calls succeed on macOS ARM64 — calling convention mismatch is x86-specific
All P/Invoke calls succeed on macOS ARM64 — calling convention mismatch is x86-specific

corrections.upstream: Root cause identified but not verified
corrections.corrected: Confirmed: 878 LibraryImport declarations with 0 UnmanagedCallConv attributes in SkiaApi.generated.cs
